Solving for Linear Equation

slides.title: Introduction to Linear Equations
slides.html:
Core concept

Understand this definition — every example and practice problem in this lesson builds on it.

Definition: A linear equation in one variable is an equation that can be written in the form:

\[ ax + b = 0 \]

where \( a \) and \( b \) are constants, and \( x \) is the variable.

Key Characteristics:

  • The highest exponent of \( x \) is 1, making it a first-degree equation.
  • The solution to a linear equation is the value of \( x \) that makes the equation true.
  • The equation represents a straight line when graphed on the coordinate plane.
slides.study_tip: Focus on definitions and vocabulary — SAT questions often test whether you recognize the form of an equation.

slides.title: Example: Solve for x
slides.html:
Solve the equation:

\[ 2x - 5 = 9 \]
✦
Your turnWork it out on paper first — reveal when you're ready.

Solution:

\[\begin{aligned} 2x - 5 &= 9 \\ 2x &= 14 \\ x &= 7 \end{aligned}\]
slides.study_tip: Watch the method, then close the solution and redo the problem from scratch.
slides.strategy_hint: Identify what type of linear equation this is (one solution, none, or infinite) before solving.

slides.title: Up next Three Types of Solutions
slides.html:
Up next

Three Types of Solutions

slides.title: Three Types of Solutions
slides.html:
Three Types of Solutions:

  • One Solution: The equation simplifies to \( x = c \), where \( c \) is a constant. Example:
\[ 2x + 3 = 7 \quad \Rightarrow \quad x = 2 \]
  • No Solution: The equation simplifies to a contradiction, such as \( 0 = -3 \), which is false. Example:
\[ 3x + 2 = 3x + 5 \quad \Rightarrow \quad 2 = 5 \quad (false) \]
  • Infinite Solutions: The equation simplifies to an identity, such as \( 0 = 0 \), meaning any \( x \) is a solution. Example:
\[ 4x + 2 = 2(2x + 1) \quad \Rightarrow \quad 4x + 2 = 4x + 2 \]
slides.study_tip: Take notes on anything that feels new — you'll reuse these ideas in practice sets.

slides.title: Example 3: No Solution Case
slides.html:
Consider the equation:

\[ 3(kx + 13) = \frac{48}{17}x + 36 \]

Given that the equation has no solution, find \( k \).

✦
Your turnWork it out on paper first — reveal when you're ready.

Solution:

\[\begin{aligned} 3kx + 39 &= \frac{48}{17}x + 36 \\ x(3k - \frac{48}{17}) &= -3 \end{aligned}\]

Since there is no solution, the coefficient of \( x \) must be zero:

\[ 3k - \frac{48}{17} = 0 \]

Solving for \( k \):

\[ k = \frac{16}{17} \]
slides.study_tip: Watch the method, then close the solution and redo the problem from scratch.
slides.strategy_hint: Set the coefficients of x equal, then verify the constants produce a contradiction.

slides.title: Algebra Problem: No Solution Condition
slides.html:
?
Try it yourselfPause here and attempt the problem before viewing the solution.

Problem Statement:

Given the equation:

\[ 2(kx - n) = (-\frac{28}{15}) x - \frac{36}{19} \]

where \( k \) and \( n \) are constants, and \( n > 1 \). The equation has no solution.

Question: What is the value of \( k \)?
slides.study_tip: No-solution means parallel lines: same slope (coefficient of x), different constants.
slides.strategy_hint: Set the coefficients of x equal, then verify the constants produce a contradiction.

slides.title: Solution: Finding k
slides.html:
Walk through the solution

Step 1: Expand both sides

Expanding the left-hand side:

\[ 2kx - 2n \]

The right-hand side remains:

\[ -\frac{28}{15} x - \frac{36}{19} \]

Step 2: Compare coefficients of \( x \)

Since the equation has no solution, the coefficients of \( x \) must be equal, but the constants must create a contradiction.

\[ 2k = -\frac{28}{15} \]

Step 3: Solve for \( k \)

\[ k = \frac{-28}{30} = \frac{-14}{15} \]

Final Answer: \( k = -\frac{14}{15} \)
slides.study_tip: Walk through each step and ask: why is this move valid? Could you explain it to a friend?

slides.title: Question
slides.html:
Consider the equation:

\[ 9x + 5 = a(x + b) \]

where \( a \) and \( b \) are constants. The equation has no solutions.

Which of the following must be true?

  1. \( a = 9 \)
  2. \( b = 5 \)
  3. \( b \neq \frac{5}{9} \)

Answer Choices:

Select your answer, then check when ready.
slides.study_tip: No-solution means parallel lines: same slope (coefficient of x), different constants.
slides.strategy_hint: Set the coefficients of x equal, then verify the constants produce a contradiction.

slides.title: Solution
slides.html:
Walk through the solution

Step 1: Understanding the Equation

  • The given equation is:
    \[ 9x + 5 = a(x + b) \]
  • For the equation to have no solution, both sides must be parallel, meaning:
    \[ 9 = a, \quad b \neq \frac{5}{9} \]

Step 2: Answer Choices

  • \( a = 9 \) must be true (Statement I is correct).
  • \( b = 5 \) is not necessarily true (Statement II is incorrect).
  • \( b \neq \frac{5}{9} \) must be true (Statement III is correct).

Final Answer: Option D (I and III only).
slides.study_tip: Walk through each step and ask: why is this move valid? Could you explain it to a friend?

slides.title: Up next Real World Application
slides.html:
Up next

Real World Application

slides.title: Word Problem: Corn Removal Rate
slides.html:
?
Try it yourselfPause here and attempt the problem before viewing the solution.

Problem Statement:

Hector used an auger to remove corn from a storage bin at a constant rate. The bin contained 24,000 bushels of corn when Hector began to use the auger. After 5 hours, 19,350 bushels remained in the bin. If the auger continues to remove corn at this rate, what is the total number of hours Hector will have been using the auger when 12,840 bushels remain?

Answer Choices:

A3
B7
C8
D12
slides.study_tip: Word problem flow: define a variable → write the equation → solve → check the units.
slides.strategy_hint: Find the rate of change first (Δy/Δx), then build the linear model.

slides.title: Solution: Finding the Total Hours
slides.html:
Walk through the solution

Step 1: Find the rate of corn removal

  • Initial amount of corn: \( 24,000 \) bushels
  • Remaining after 5 hours: \( 19,350 \) bushels
  • Corn removed in 5 hours:
\[ 24,000 - 19,350 = 4,650 bushels \]
  • Rate of removal:
\[ \frac{4,650}{5} = 930 bushels per hour \]

Step 2: Find the total hours needed for 12,840 bushels remaining

  • Find the total corn removed:
\[ 24,000 - 12,840 = 11,160 bushels \]
  • Find the total hours:
\[ \frac{11,160}{930} = 12 hours \]

Final Answer: D. 12
slides.study_tip: Walk through each step and ask: why is this move valid? Could you explain it to a friend?

slides.title: Glenview Street Property Discount
slides.html:
Table: Townsend Realty Group Investments

Property addressPurchase price ($)Monthly rental price ($)
Clearwater Lane128,000950
Driftwood Drive176,0001,310
Edgemont Street70,000515
Glenview Street140,0001,040
Hamilton Circle450,0003,365

Townsend Realty Group purchased the Glenview Street property and received a 40% discount off the original price along with an additional 20% off the discounted price for purchasing in cash.

Which of the following best approximates the original price, in dollars, of the Glenview Street property?

A$350,000
B$291,700
C$233,300
D$175,000
slides.study_tip: Word problem flow: define a variable → write the equation → solve → check the units.
slides.strategy_hint: Translate each percentage change into a multiplier, then chain the operations.

slides.title: Solution: Glenview Street Property
slides.html:
Let \(\displaystyle P\) be the original price of the Glenview Street property.

  • First, a 40% discount was applied:
    \[ P \times 0.60 \]
  • Then, an additional 20% discount was applied to the new value:
    \[ P \times 0.60 \times 0.80 = 0.48P \]
  • We are told the final purchase price was $140,000:
    \[ 0.48P = 140,000 \Rightarrow P = \frac{140,000}{0.48} = 291,666.67 \]

Answer: B. 291,700
slides.study_tip: Walk through each step and ask: why is this move valid? Could you explain it to a friend?

slides.title: Up next Practice
slides.html:
Up next

Practice

slides.title: Question : Infinitely Many Solutions
slides.html:
?
Try it yourselfPause here and attempt the problem before viewing the solution.

Given:

\[ \frac{12x + 28}{4} - \frac{s}{13} = r(x - 8) \]

where \(s\) and \(r\) are constants, and \(s > 0\).

If the equation has infinitely many solutions, what is the value of \(s\)?
slides.study_tip: Infinite solutions mean the two sides are the same line — all coefficients must match.
slides.strategy_hint: Match coefficients of x and constants on both sides — they must be identical.

slides.title: Answer: Infinitely Many Solutions
slides.html:
Walk through the solution

Step 1: Simplify the left side

\[ \frac{12x + 28}{4} = 3x + 7 \quad \Rightarrow \quad 3x + 7 - \frac{s}{13} \]

So the equation becomes:

\[ 3x + 7 - \frac{s}{13} = r(x - 8) \]

Step 2: Expand the right side

\[ r(x - 8) = rx - 8r \]

Step 3: For infinitely many solutions, both sides must be identical

\[\begin{aligned} Match coefficients: \quad & 3 = r \quad \Rightarrow r = 3 \\ Match constants: \quad & 7 - \frac{s}{13} = -8r = -24 \\ & \Rightarrow \frac{s}{13} = 31 \Rightarrow s = 403 \end{aligned}\]

Correct Answer: \( s = 403 \)
slides.study_tip: Walk through each step and ask: why is this move valid? Could you explain it to a friend?

slides.title: Question: No Solution Linear Equation
slides.html:
?
Try it yourselfPause here and attempt the problem before viewing the solution.

Given the equation:

\[ \frac{1}{3}(bx + c) = \frac{14}{27}x + \frac{4}{9} \]

where \(b\) and \(c\) are constants and \(c > 3\).

The equation has no solution. What is the value of \(b\)?
slides.study_tip: No-solution means parallel lines: same slope (coefficient of x), different constants.
slides.strategy_hint: Set the coefficients of x equal, then verify the constants produce a contradiction.

slides.title: Answer: No Solution Condition
slides.html:
Walk through the solution

Step 1: Expand the left-hand side:

\[ \frac{1}{3}(bx + c) = \frac{b}{3}x + \frac{c}{3} \]

Now the equation becomes:

\[ \frac{b}{3}x + \frac{c}{3} = \frac{14}{27}x + \frac{4}{9} \]

Step 2: For a linear equation to have no solution, the coefficients of \(x\) must be equal but the constants must be different.

Match the coefficients:

\[ \frac{b}{3} = \frac{14}{27} \Rightarrow b = \frac{14}{27} \cdot 3 = \frac{14}{9} \]

Check the constants:

\[ \frac{c}{3} \neq \frac{4}{9}, \quad and c > 3 \Rightarrow This guarantees different constants \]

Final Answer: \(b = \frac{14}{9}\)
slides.study_tip: Walk through each step and ask: why is this move valid? Could you explain it to a friend?

slides.title: Question: No Solution for Linear Equation
slides.html:
?
Try it yourselfPause here and attempt the problem before viewing the solution.

Given the equation:

\[ ax - b = 3(2x + 1) \]

where \(a\) and \(b\) are constants, and the equation has no solution.

Which of the following could be the values of \(a\) and \(b\)?

  • A. \(a = 2\), \(b = -3\)
  • B. \(a = 2\), \(b = 3\)
  • C. \(a = 6\), \(b = -3\)
  • D. \(a = 6\), \(b = 3\)
slides.study_tip: No-solution means parallel lines: same slope (coefficient of x), different constants.
slides.strategy_hint: Set the coefficients of x equal, then verify the constants produce a contradiction.

slides.title: Answer: No Solution Condition
slides.html:
Walk through the solution

Step 1: Expand the right-hand side

\[ ax - b = 6x + 3 \]

For no solution:

Coefficients of \(x\) must match, but constants must not:

\[ a = 6, \quad -b \neq 3 \Rightarrow b \neq -3 \]

Now test options:

  • A. \(a = 2\), not valid
  • B. \(a = 2\), not valid
  • C. \(a = 6\), \(b = -3\) → \(-b = 3\), so both sides match → infinite solutions
  • D. \(a = 6\), \(b = 3\) → \(-b = -3 \neq 3\), valid: no solution

Correct Answer: \(D\)
slides.study_tip: Walk through each step and ask: why is this move valid? Could you explain it to a friend?

slides.title: Question: Absolute Value Equation
slides.html:
?
Try it yourselfPause here and attempt the problem before viewing the solution.

Solve for the positive solution to the equation:

\[ 3|5 - x| = 42 - 4|5 - x| \]
slides.study_tip: Absolute value equations often split into two cases — remember to check both branches.
slides.strategy_hint: Isolate |expression| first, then split into two linear equations.

slides.title: Answer: Solving Absolute Value Equation
slides.html:
Walk through the solution

Step 1: Let \(y = |5 - x|\), then:

\[ 3y = 42 - 4y \Rightarrow 7y = 42 \Rightarrow y = 6 \]

Step 2: Solve \(|5 - x| = 6\)

\[ 5 - x = 6 \Rightarrow x = -1 \quad or \quad 5 - x = -6 \Rightarrow x = 11 \]

Step 3: Positive solution: \(11\)
slides.study_tip: Walk through each step and ask: why is this move valid? Could you explain it to a friend?

slides.title: Question: Popcorn Word Problem
slides.html:
?
Try it yourselfPause here and attempt the problem before viewing the solution.

Joey and Matt were tasked with bringing popcorn to a certain event. Joey brought \(x\) pounds of popcorn and Matt brought 4 fewer pounds than Joey did.

During the event, 60% of the popcorn they had brought was consumed.

If Joey and Matt divided the leftover popcorn evenly between them, which of the following represents the number of pounds of leftover popcorn each of them took?

  • A. \(0.2x - 0.8\)
  • B. \(0.4x - 0.8\)
  • C. \(0.6x - 1.2\)
  • D. \(0.8x - 1.6\)
slides.study_tip: Word problem flow: define a variable → write the equation → solve → check the units.
slides.strategy_hint: Find the rate of change first (Δy/Δx), then build the linear model.

slides.title: Answer: Popcorn Division
slides.html:
Walk through the solution

Step 1: Total popcorn brought

\[ Joey: x, \quad Matt: x - 4 \Rightarrow Total: x + (x - 4) = 2x - 4 \]

Step 2: 60% consumed → 40% remaining

\[ Leftover: 0.4(2x - 4) = 0.8x - 1.6 \]

Step 3: Divided evenly between 2 people

\[ \frac{0.8x - 1.6}{2} = 0.4x - 0.8 \]

Final Answer: \(B. 0.4x - 0.8\)
slides.study_tip: Walk through each step and ask: why is this move valid? Could you explain it to a friend?

slides.title: Summary
slides.html:
◆
Lesson recapReview these ideas before moving to practice tests.
  • A linear equation in one variable has one solution, no solution, or infinitely many solutions.
  • Contradictions (e.g., \( 0 = -3 \)) mean no solution.
  • Identities (e.g., \( 0 = 0 \)) mean infinite solutions.
slides.study_tip: Quick recap — make sure you can recall each bullet without looking.
